question_text: Let $\{ X _ { n } \} _ { n \geq 0 }$ be a discrete time Markov chain on the state space {1,2,3} with one-step transition probability matrix $ \begin{array} { c c c c } { { } } & { { 1 } } & { { 2 } } & { { 3 } } \\ { { 1 } } & { { \left[ 0 . 4 \right. } } & { { 0 . 3 } } & { { 0 . 3 } } \\ { { 2 } } & { { \left[ 0 . 5 } } & { { 0 . 2 } } & { { 0 . 3 \right] } } \\ { { 3 } } & { { \left[ 0 . 2 } } & { { 0 . 4 } } & { { 0 . 4 \right] } } \end{array} $

options.A: nd initial distribution $P ( X _ { 0 } = 1 ) = 0 . 5 , P ( X _ { 0 } = 2 ) = 0 . 2 , P ( X _ { 0 } = 3 ) = 0 . 3$ Then $P ( X _ { 1 } = 2 , X _ { 2 } = 3 , X _ { 3 } = 1 )$ (rounded off to three decimal places) is equal to .

solution:
To find the probability \( P(X_1 = 2, X_2 = 3, X_3 = 1) \), we need to use the properties of the Markov chain. The Markov property tells us that the probability of a sequence of states depends only on the current state and not on the entire history of the process. Therefore, we can break down the probability into a product of one-step transition probabilities.

Given the initial distribution:
\[ P(X_0 = 1) = 0.5, \quad P(X_0 = 2) = 0.2, \quad P(X_0 = 3) = 0.3 \]

We need to find \( P(X_1 = 2, X_2 = 3, X_3 = 1) \). Using the Markov property, we can write:
\[ P(X_1 = 2, X_2 = 3, X_3 = 1) = P(X_0 = i) \cdot P(X_1 = 2 \mid X_0 = i) \cdot P(X_2 = 3 \mid X_1 = 2) \cdot P(X_3 = 1 \mid X_2 = 3) \]

We need to consider all possible values of \( i \) (which can be 1, 2, or 3) and sum the probabilities for each case.

### Case 1: \( i = 1 \)
\[ P(X_0 = 1) \cdot P(X_1 = 2 \mid X_0 = 1) \cdot P(X_2 = 3 \mid X_1 = 2) \cdot P(X_3 = 1 \mid X_2 = 3) \]
\[ = 0.5 \cdot 0.3 \cdot 0.3 \cdot 0.2 \]
\[ = 0.5 \times 0.3 \times 0.3 \times 0.2 \]
\[ = 0.009 \]

### Case 2: \( i = 2 \)
\[ P(X_0 = 2) \cdot P(X_1 = 2 \mid X_0 = 2) \cdot P(X_2 = 3 \mid X_1 = 2) \cdot P(X_3 = 1 \mid X_2 = 3) \]
\[ = 0.2 \cdot 0.2 \cdot 0.3 \cdot 0.2 \]
\[ = 0.2 \times 0.2 \times 0.3 \times 0.2 \]
\[ = 0.0024 \]

### Case 3: \( i = 3 \)
\[ P(X_0 = 3) \cdot P(X_1 = 2 \mid X_0 = 3) \cdot P(X_2 = 3 \mid X_1 = 2) \cdot P(X_3 = 1 \mid X_2 = 3) \]
\[ = 0.3 \cdot 0.4 \cdot 0.3 \cdot 0.2 \]
\[ = 0.3 \times 0.4 \times 0.3
